name: Peano Arithmetic & Paris — Part 9: Paris-Harrington Theorem
description: The Paris-Harrington theorem: a strengthened finite Ramsey theorem is true but unprovable in PA. The key: every k-coloring of [N]^n has a homogeneous subset Y with |Y| ≥ min(Y) (relatively large). Proof: PA ⊢ PH ⟹ Con(PA); by Gödel's Second Incompleteness, PA ⊬ Con(PA), so PA ⊬ PH.
mermaid:
graph TD
    RefSecondIncomp["Ref: Second Incompleteness (Part 8)"]
    DefFinRamsey["Def: Finite Ramsey: R(n,k,m) = least N s.t. every k-coloring of [N]^n has homogeneous m-set"]
    DefRelLarge["Def: Y relatively large iff |Y| ≥ min(Y)"]
    DefPH["Def: PH(n,k,m): strengthened Ramsey with relatively large homogeneous set"]
    LemFinRamseyPA["Lem: Finite Ramsey theorem provable in PA"]
    LemPHFromInf["Lem: PH follows from infinite Ramsey (compactness) or transfinite induction"]
    LemPHTrue["Lem: PH is true in standard model ℕ"]
    LemPHImplCon["Lem: PA ⊢ PH(n,k,m) ⟹ Con(PA); formalized proof"]
    ThmParisHarrington["Thm: Paris-Harrington: PH unprovable in PA"]
    CorPHProvable["Cor: PH provable in ACA₀, ZFC, or transfinite induction"]
    CorPHFast["Cor: PH Ramsey function grows faster than any PA-provably total function"]
    RefSecondIncomp --> LemPHImplCon
    RefSecondIncomp --> ThmParisHarrington
    DefFinRamsey --> DefPH
    DefRelLarge --> DefPH
    DefPH --> LemFinRamseyPA
    DefPH --> LemPHFromInf
    LemPHFromInf --> LemPHTrue
    LemPHTrue --> LemPHImplCon
    LemPHImplCon --> ThmParisHarrington
    ThmParisHarrington --> CorPHProvable
    ThmParisHarrington --> CorPHFast
    classDef definition fill:#b197fc,color:#fff,stroke:#9775fa
    classDef lemma fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff,stroke:#4dabf7
    classDef theorem fill:#51cf66,color:#fff,stroke:#40c057
    classDef corollary fill:#1abc9c,color:#fff,stroke:#16a085
    classDef ref fill:#bdc3c7,color:#333,stroke:#95a5a6
    class RefSecondIncomp ref
    class DefFinRamsey,DefRelLarge,DefPH definition
    class LemFinRamseyPA,LemPHFromInf,LemPHTrue,LemPHImplCon lemma
    class ThmParisHarrington theorem
    class CorPHProvable,CorPHFast corollary
